topics.title: Musical Performance
topics.summary: The show consists entirely of musical segments.

topics.title: J.D.'s Social Skills and Upcoming Vegas Trip
topics.summary: The studio features a conversation regarding J.D.'s lack of social confidence during a night out in Hoboken and the group's plans for an upcoming trip to Las Vegas.

topics.title: Diner Date and Fan Encounters
topics.summary: The studio explores a bizarre date involving a fan's request for an autograph and Howard's experience with a persistent restaurant owner.

topics.title: Bionic-Con and Celebrity Interactions
topics.summary: The studio explores the absurdity of a fan convention where actor Lee Majors failed to appear, discusses various headlines from The Onion, and reacts to a listener's offer to perform an outrageous task for a radio appearance.

topics.title: Radio Show Interactions and Hospital Update
topics.summary: The studio features a call from a listener with learning disabilities, followed by an update on a colleague hospitalized for surgery and discussions regarding future radio segments.

topics.title: Technology and Personal Life
topics.summary: The studio explores the features of the iPhone, compares mobile phone experiences with various brands, and discusses a guest's personal life and professional challenges.

topics.title: Artie's Mental Health Intervention
topics.summary: The studio hosts and a psychiatrist discuss Artie's avoidance of therapy, his struggles with weight and depression, and the importance of confronting personal issues rather than using work as a distraction.

topics.title: Therapeutic Exploration of Childhood Trauma
topics.summary: The studio explores a guest's internal conflicts regarding his personal growth, the impact of his father's parenting style on his current psyche, and his fear of losing his comedic identity during the therapeutic process.

topics.title: Personal Relationships and Media Stories
topics.summary: The studio explores personal anecdotes regarding family dynamics, a reporter's experience with a toxic insect in Mexico, and the story of an elderly chimpanzee who creates artwork.

topics.title: Iron Sheik and Wrestling Commentary
topics.summary: The studio features a collection of wrestling-related clips, including segments featuring the Iron Sheik, various song parodies, and humorous exchanges regarding professional wrestling figures.

topics.title: Leslie West's Career and Drug History
topics.summary: The studio features Leslie West discussing his history with heroin addiction, his work on The Who's album "Who's Next," and the criteria for induction into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame.

topics.title: Musical Comparisons and Humming
topics.summary: The studio features a discussion regarding the musical prowess of various bands followed by an extended period of humming to the song "Little Robin Marie."

topics.title: High-Pitch Eric's Apartment Conditions
topics.summary: The studio discusses the unsanitary conditions and reports of a foul odor emanating from high-pitch Eric's apartment, which is reportedly filled with animal waste and debris.

topics.title: Studio Incidents and Celebrity News
topics.summary: The studio discusses internal workplace mishaps involving staff members, upcoming segments on the show, and various celebrity news stories including political excerpts and sports dilemmas.

topics.title: Celebrity News and Current Events
topics.summary: The show covers a variety of topics including Joey Buttafuco's transition to radio, the appearance of David Beckham and Victoria Beckham in Hollywood, casting news for a Batman film, and the passing of Tammy Faye Bakker.

topics.title: Televangelism, Sports Scandals, and Pop Culture
topics.summary: The studio discusses the death of a televangelist, an NBA referee involved in a gambling scandal, Barry Bonds' record pursuit, and upcoming entertainment news including The Simpsons movie and country music performances.
